method: Stacked bar chart with product categories on X-axis and total sales quantity on Y-axis. Time-of-day patterns represented as color-stacked segments (e.g., morning/afternoon/evening) derived from order_date timestamps. Linked to I1 for category brushing.
objective: Identify top-selling categories and their temporal sales patterns to optimize inventory allocation and delivery scheduling

method: Toggleable bar chart (per I2) showing category-level revenue sum (primary mode) vs margin-weighted revenue calculated as ∑(order_total × margin_percentage). Color encodes performance quartiles relative to selected metric.
objective: Compare raw revenue contribution vs profitability-adjusted performance across product categories

method: Scatterplot matrix with price-quantity pairs per category, trend lines showing elasticity. Bubble size represents total revenue (price×quantity). Category brush (I1) filters matrix subplots.
objective: Analyze price sensitivity and demand elasticity patterns across product categories

method: Grouped bar chart (V4) with customer segments on X-axis and error bars showing ±1.5 IQR ranges. Bar height represents mean order value. Click interactions filter co-occurring views via shared segment parameter.
objective: Compare spending patterns across customer segments to inform loyalty program adjustments

method: Line chart overlay: x-axis=time, y1=stock received (inventory), y2=sales quantity (orders). Category brush (I1) highlights corresponding lines. Anomalies detected via stock-out events (y2 > y1).
objective: Identify inventory replenishment needs through temporal alignment of stock levels and sales volume

method: Histogram with price ratios (unit_price/mrp) across categories. Anomaly thresholds marked at <0.85 and >1.0 ratios. Category brush (I1) enables focused outlier inspection.
objective: Detect pricing irregularities and potential MAP policy violations
